data.title: Electromed is a new Buy at B. Riley; Buyout potential cited
data.description:
B. Riley launched its coverage of Electromed (OTCQB:EMED) with a Buy recommendation and a $38 per share target, arguing that the company, being the final remaining pure-play stock in a market for a type of airway clearance products, is a potential takeout target.

The New Prague, Minnesota-based firm offers high-frequency chest wall oscillation (HFCWO) airway clearance products targeting patients with respiratory disorders such as COPD in a market worth about $240M in annual sales, according to B. Riley’s Kyle Bauser.

With its rivals being healthcare conglomerates such as Baxter (BAX) and Phillips (PHG), EMED “is the final remaining pure-play in the HFCWO space, making it an attractive takeout target, in our opinion, for a larger medtech strategy looking to leverage the pulmonologist call point,” Bauser said.

Additionally, the analyst expects the company to continue its double-digit revenue growth and maintain its sizable operating leverage in the pursuit of $1.00 of per share earnings by fiscal 2026 and 20% operating margins over time.

data.title: Crude inventory rises by 4.6M barrels for week ended Feb 14 – EIA
data.description:
Commercial crude stocks for the week ended February 14: 432.5M barrels.

Crude inventory change: +4.6M barrels vs. +4.1M barrels for the week ended Feb. 7. Consensus estimate 3.200M.

Futures (CL1:COM) +1.05% to $72.86 a barrel.

ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (SCO), (BNO), (DBO), (USL).

data.title: Salesforce CEO Benioff denies report company in talks with others to handle AI
data.description:
• Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) CEO Marc Benioff took to social media to deny a report that the cloud computing software giant was in talks with several tech firms for a major new cloud agreement in an effort to handle its artificial intelligence workload.
  • “The story in @TheInformation is incorrect,” Benioff posted on X. “In 2024, Salesforce explored a fourth deployment option for our customers, alongside our existing choices: our proprietary data centers, AWS, and Alibaba. Last year, we decided to extend our partnership with Google, giving customers the option to deploy Salesforce’s customers 360 apps, Hyperforce, Agentforce, and Data Cloud on Google’s platform in future Salesforce releases.”
  • The news outlet reported that Salesforce was in negotiations with Microsoft (MSFT), Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) and Oracle (ORCL) about a major new cloud agreement, citing an interview with Srini Tallapragada, Salesforce’s president and chief engineering officer. The news outlet added that the supposed deal would be worth more than $1B over numerous years.
  • Salesforce shares fell 1.8% in late morning trading on Thursday.

data.title: Lloyds Banking declares final dividend of 2.11 pence/share, announces £1.7 billion buyback
data.description:
• Lloyds Banking (NYSE:LYG) board has recommended a final ordinary dividend of 2.11 pence per share, which, together with the interim ordinary dividend of 1.06 pence per share totals 3.17 pence per share, up 15% from 2.76 pence per share.
  • Payable May 20; for shareholders of record April 11; ex-div April 11.
  • The Board has also announced to buyback ordinary share of up to £1.7 billion, which will commence as soon as is practicable and is expected to be completed by 31 December 2025.
  • See LYG Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Endava projects FY2025 revenue of £795M-£800M with focus on AI and core modernization
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Endava plc (NYSE:DAVA) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO John Cotterell highlighted the ongoing shift toward AI-driven digital transformation, referring to it as the "digital shift." He emphasized the company's ability to leverage AI-enabled solutions like Morpheus and Compass to meet growing client demand. Cotterell noted, "Gen AI adoption is becoming a key priority for clients," and expressed confidence in Endava's ability to deliver enterprise-level solutions.

  • The company announced its first share buyback program of $100M, contingent on shareholder approval, as part of its strategy to optimize capital allocation.

  • Endava reported strong growth in its banking and capital markets vertical, with a 43.6% increase year-over-year. Cotterell highlighted recent client wins, including a North American financial institution's core modernization project and a multi-year partnership with a European commodities trader.

  • CFO Mark Thurston noted that adjusted diluted EPS for Q2 was £0.30, exceeding the previous guidance of £0.24-£0.25, driven by improved cost management. He added that the adjusted PBT margin improved to 11.2% compared to Q1's 9.9%, reflecting optimization efforts.

Outlook

  • For Q3 fiscal year 2025, Endava expects revenue between £198M and £200M, representing 13%-14% constant currency growth year-over-year. Adjusted diluted EPS is projected to be £0.31-£0.32.

  • Full-year fiscal 2025 revenue guidance was adjusted to £795M-£800M, reflecting constant currency growth of 8.5%-9%, with adjusted diluted EPS expected at £1.20-£1.23. The guidance assumes exchange rates of £1 to $1.24 and €1.20 as of January 31, 2025.

  • Thurston pointed to increased softness in the UK and rest of the world regions, attributing it to a worsening macroeconomic environment. He noted that these challenges have led to project ramp-downs and pipeline delays.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q2 fiscal 2025 totaled £195.6M, up 6.6% year-over-year and 9.1% in constant currency. North America accounted for 39% of revenue, showing 32.7% growth compared to the previous year, driven by the GalaxE acquisition.

  • Profit before tax declined to £2.5M from £10.6M in the prior year, while adjusted PBT was £21.8M. Revenue from the 10 largest clients accounted for 36% of total revenue, with average client spend increasing 13% year-over-year.

  • Adjusted free cash flow was reported at £31.6M for the quarter, with cash and cash equivalents totaling £60.1M. Borrowings decreased to £123.7M from £144.8M as of June 30, 2024.

Q&A

  • Zachary Ajzenman, TD Cowen: Asked about assumptions for Q3 guidance and sequential acceleration in Q4. CEO Cotterell explained that large core modernization deals, while slower to ramp, are expected to contribute to revenue growth later in the fiscal year. CFO Thurston added that macroeconomic challenges in the UK and rest of the world regions were factored into the guidance.

  • Tyler DuPont, Bank of America Merrill Lynch: Inquired about visibility into 2025 budgets. Cotterell noted that many clients are still finalizing budgets, with some reshuffling to accommodate AI-driven core modernization projects.

  • Jonathan Lee, Guggenheim Partners: Asked about the impact of recent project ramp-downs. Thurston explained that these were broad-based and primarily driven by macroeconomic uncertainty in specific geographies.

  • Antonio Jaramillo, Morgan Stanley: Sought an update on the GalaxE integration. Thurston stated that the process is well-progressed, with system cutovers expected by the end of February 2025. He highlighted ongoing cost optimization efforts.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on the potential of AI and core modernization projects but raising concerns about visibility and macroeconomic headwinds. The tone was slightly skeptical during discussions about pipeline delays and project ramp-downs.

  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks but was more cautious and detailed in addressing analyst concerns. Cotterell repeatedly emphasized the long-term potential of AI-driven initiatives, while Thurston acknowledged macroeconomic challenges.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone showed increased confidence in cost optimization and AI-driven opportunities but more caution regarding near-term macroeconomic impacts.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for Q3 revenue increased slightly from Q2, while full-year revenue projections were narrowed to reflect macroeconomic headwinds.

  • Management's tone in the current quarter was more focused on AI and core modernization, compared to the previous quarter's broader emphasis on digital transformation.

  • Analyst concerns shifted from overall market dynamics to specific macroeconomic challenges and delays in client decision-making.

  • Adjusted PBT margin improved quarter-over-quarter, reflecting ongoing cost control and efficiency efforts.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted macroeconomic uncertainty in the UK and rest of the world regions, leading to client caution and project delays.

  • Analyst concerns centered on elongated sales cycles for large deals and potential volatility in top client spending.

  • Mitigation strategies include focusing on cost optimization, leveraging AI-driven accelerators, and deepening client relationships through long-term partnerships.

Final Takeaway

Endava plc is navigating a challenging macroeconomic environment while capitalizing on the growing demand for AI-driven core modernization solutions. The company remains optimistic about its long-term growth potential, supported by strategic client wins, cost optimization efforts, and a focus on high-demand areas like AI and cloud. However, near-term visibility is impacted by elongated sales cycles and regional economic challenges, which are reflected in a cautious outlook for the remainder of fiscal 2025.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: EPAM Systems targets 10-14% revenue growth in 2025 with GenAI investments
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: EPAM Systems (NYSE:EPAM) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Arkadiy Dobkin highlighted better-than-expected Q4 results, emphasizing the company's return to organic revenue growth for the first time since Q1 2023. He attributed the success to core engineering capabilities and advanced service offerings, particularly in AI-related capabilities.

  • Dobkin discussed the acquisition of NEORIS and First Derivative, which expanded EPAM's global footprint by nearly 6,000 employees across Latin America, Canada, Spain, UK, and Ireland. These acquisitions aim to penetrate new markets and enhance talent hubs.

  • CFO Jason Peterson reported Q4 revenue of $1.25 billion, driven by stronger-than-expected project starts and organic growth in key verticals, including life sciences, healthcare, and financial services.

  • Peterson stated: "Our non-GAAP diluted EPS was $2.84, reflecting an increase of $0.09 or 3.3% compared to the same quarter in 2023."

  • Dobkin emphasized the growing momentum in GenAI initiatives, noting that 75% of the top clients are engaged in GenAI programs. He highlighted $50 million in revenue from AI-native programs in Q4 alone.

Outlook

  • Management projects 2025 revenue growth between 10% and 14%, with approximately 10% coming from inorganic contributions. Non-GAAP income from operations is expected to range from 14.5% to 15.5%.

  • For Q1 2025, revenue is expected to range from $1.275 billion to $1.290 billion, reflecting 10% year-over-year growth, with non-GAAP income from operations between 12.5% and 13.5%.

  • Despite optimism, management remains cautious about macroeconomic risks and geopolitical uncertainties. Dobkin noted, "2025 will be a challenging and transformative year for the industry."

Financial Results

  • Q4 revenue was $1.25 billion, up 7.9% year-over-year, including contributions from recent acquisitions. Organic revenue grew 1% year-over-year on a constant currency basis.

  • Financial services saw a 15.9% year-over-year increase, supported by 4.3% organic and 11.6% inorganic growth. Life sciences and healthcare also grew strongly, increasing by 8.6% year-over-year.

  • Gross margin declined slightly to 30.4% on a GAAP basis, impacted by compensation increases and lower profitability from recent acquisitions.

  • Free cash flow reached $115 million, down from $161 million in Q4 2023, primarily due to acquisition-related expenses.

Q&A

  • Maggie Nolan, William Blair: Asked about the assumptions behind revenue guidance. CFO Peterson explained, "We are seeing substantial program starts and clients beginning to really get started here in 2025."

  • Jamie Friedman, Susquehanna: Asked about pricing pressure on "run the business" versus "change the business" initiatives. Dobkin noted that pricing pressure persists across both, but transformational programs are starting to garner improved pricing.

  • Bryan Bergin, TD Cowen: Inquired about new client dynamics. Dobkin revealed that many clients returning to EPAM are driven by the company's quality of service and expertise in GenAI programs.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, with questions focusing on pricing pressures, integration of acquisitions, and macroeconomic risks. Concerns about wage inflation and pricing dynamics were recurring themes.

  • Management maintained a slightly positive tone, emphasizing strategic investments in talent retention and GenAI capabilities. Prepared remarks were confident, but responses in the Q&A displayed caution regarding near-term profitability and geopolitical risks.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management showed incremental optimism, citing improved demand signals and stabilization in key markets.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 marked a stronger return to organic revenue growth compared to Q3, where growth was flat. The acquisitions of NEORIS and First Derivative added significant inorganic contributions, particularly in Latin America and Europe.

  • Management's tone in Q4 reflected greater confidence in the mid-term outlook, driven by improving client sentiment and increased demand for GenAI programs. This was a shift from Q3's more cautious optimism.

  • Analysts' focus shifted from stabilization in Q3 to probing deeper into profitability challenges and the integration of acquisitions in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged macroeconomic uncertainties and geopolitical risks, particularly in Europe and Ukraine. Dobkin mentioned "multiple pockets of caution" due to dynamic geopolitical environments.

  • Wage inflation and pricing pressures remain concerns, with limited ability to offset costs through improved client pricing in the near term.

  • Analysts raised concerns about the profitability impact of recent acquisitions, noting that NEORIS and First Derivative operate at lower margins than EPAM's standalone business.

Final Takeaway

EPAM Systems' Q4 results showcased a return to organic revenue growth and strong contributions from recent acquisitions. The company's focus on GenAI initiatives, which generated $50 million in Q4 revenue, underscores its strategic emphasis on transformative technologies. While management projects 10-14% revenue growth for 2025, challenges such as pricing pressures and geopolitical risks temper their optimism. Nonetheless, EPAM's investments in talent retention and advanced capabilities position the company for long-term growth in an evolving market landscape.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: German stocks have positive catalysts amid US-Europe tensions – UBS
data.description:
German equities (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR) are best positioned to benefit despite current election volatility, according to UBS.

Solita Marcelli, chief investment officer at UBS Global Wealth Management, said in a note that German equities (NASDAQ:DAX) could benefit from higher defense spending, a manufacturing rebound, and lower gas prices despite upcoming elections and Europe’s geopolitical developments.

“We like positioning for further potential gains for the next three months via structured strategies on a pullback after the German election this weekend,” said Marcelli.

The DAX Germany Index (DAX:IND) is up 12% year-to-date, although down 0.5% on Thursday. It fell 1.8% on Wednesday as European leaders met to discuss the region’s defense spending as tensions between Europe and the U.S. rise due to President Donald Trump’s comments about President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and the Russia-Ukraine war.

President Trump called President Zelenskiy “a dictator,” and warned him that Ukraine “may be Russian someday.”

The European Union on Wednesday agreed on sanctions against Russia, including sanctions on aluminum and vessels carrying Russian oil.

UBS believes that negotiations for a ceasefire in Ukraine may be reached over the course of the year, and that Europe’s economy could see “some modest benefits amid lower energy prices and improving consumer and business sentiment.”

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join a separate political discussion.

data.title: Stocks fall, with Dow down 1% as investors balk at Walmart's guidance shortfall
data.description:
U.S. stocks dropped Thursday, with the S&P 500 retreating from a record high, in part as investors were taken aback by Walmart's (WMT) soft financial outlook.

The outlook from the world's largest retailer arrives at a time of uncertainty about the impact of tariffs on the U.S. economy, and about the Federal Reserve's path on interest rates with signs of inflation picking up.

The S&P 500 (SP500) -0.8%, and the Dow (DJI) slumped more than 600 points, or 1.4%, facing its worst percentage loss since early January. The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.9%.

Walmart (WMT) lost the most on the Dow, -6.5% after its current quarter and FY26 fell short of analyst expectations. The guidance "assumes a generally stable consumer and continued pressure from its mix of products and formats globally," Walmart said.

The Consumer Staples Sector (XLP) -1.1%, among seven other S&P 500 (SP500) sectors that fell. Other retail stocks in the red included Costco (COST), Amazon (AMZN), and Target (TGT).

Investors also considered initial jobless claims, which rose more than consensus in the past week. Continuing claims came in line. Meanwhile, the Philly Fed manufacturing index weakened more than expected M/M in February.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.51%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was lower by 1 basis point at 4.27%.

Back in equities, Palantir (PLTR) -11% after a memo from Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth called for an 8% cut to the defense budget over each of the next five years. But Wedbush Securities argued that Palantir's software approach could enable the company to gain more IT budget funds at the Pentagon.

In other waves from Washington, shares of cruise lines fell Thursday after Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick suggested that a tax status that favors certain shipping companies would end under the Trump administration. Carnival Corporation (CCL) -7.1%, Royal Caribbean (NYSE:RCL) -8.6%, and Norwegian Cruise Lines (NCLH) -6.3%.

Wall Street's major averages seesawed on Wednesday, but the benchmark S&P 500 eventually closed at a record high as investors digested the Federal Reserve's latest minutes and President Donald Trump's latest tariff action.

"The minutes of the January Federal Reserve meeting gave a cautious policy outlook. Nervousness about the economic consequences of Trump’s policies was clear. In particular, the potential for Trump to ignite a further wave of inflation was cited," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

data.title: Hydro One outlines 6-8% EPS growth through 2027 with $3.1B in 2024 capital investments
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Hydro One Limited (OTCPK:HRNNF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Hydro One's CEO David Lebeter highlighted a 10.5% increase in Q4 net income, attributed to higher revenues from OEB-approved rates and lower OM&A costs. He also announced a refreshed strategy focused on customer satisfaction, with residential and small business satisfaction scores increasing to 88%.
  • In 2024, Hydro One deployed more than $3 billion in capital, placing $2.5 billion in assets into service. The Chatham by Lakeshore Transmission project was completed one year early and $30 million under budget, marking the first 50/50 First Nations equity partnership.
  • Lebeter addressed the ongoing tariff uncertainties and highlighted collaborative efforts in Washington DC to resolve trade tensions.
  • CFO Harry Taylor cited a strong finish to 2024 with basic earnings per share of $0.33 in Q4 and annual EPS of $1.93. He emphasized $150 million in productivity savings achieved in 2024 and announced an increase in Hydro One’s EPS guidance for 2027 to a range of $2.15 to $2.37.

Outlook

  • Management expects a 6% to 8% annualized EPS growth through 2027, supported by $300 million in rural broadband rate base growth and the acquisition of a 48% stake in the East West Tie transmission line.
  • Hydro One reaffirmed its 2024 effective tax rate guidance of 13% to 16%, which remains valid for the remainder of the JRAP period.
  • The company's updated capital investments profile reflects long-term growth opportunities, including the Wawasigan Transmission Line Project and the Wawa and Porcupine transmission line.

Financial Results

  • Q4 revenue net of purchased power increased by 4.7% year-over-year. Distribution revenues rose by 11.5%, while transmission revenues saw a 0.2% decline due to lower peak demand.
  • Operating maintenance and administration (OM&A) expenses decreased by 6% in Q4, with transmission costs down 9.2% and distribution costs down 11.3%.
  • Depreciation expenses rose 14.9% year-over-year, driven by growth in fixed assets and higher asset removal costs. Interest expenses increased by 7.5% due to higher long-term debt and interest rates.
  • Hydro One raised $750 million in medium-term notes during the quarter, bringing the total for 2024 to over $2.75 billion.

Q&A

  • James Ward, Jefferies LLC: Asked about interplay between broadband investments and future rate base growth. CFO Harry Taylor stated that productivity savings and new transmission lines would offset regulatory resets, ensuring earnings growth momentum into the next rate period.
  • Maurice Choy, RBC Capital Markets: Questioned potential changes to the cost of capital review. CEO Lebeter indicated no clear signals of increases or decreases but noted the opportunity to propose adjustments in the next rate application.
  • Benjamin Pham, BMO: Inquired about the long-term growth potential beyond 2027. Taylor affirmed that growth drivers include population expansion, C&I developments, and new transmission projects, though broadband growth would be limited after rural areas are connected.
  • Robert Hope, Scotiabank: Asked about productivity gains amid inflationary pressures. Taylor emphasized Hydro One's ongoing initiatives to drive productivity and offset inflation through operational improvements and technology.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a cautiously optimistic tone, focusing on broadband, cost of capital reviews, and long-term growth. Questions were probing but not adversarial, with a noticeable focus on potential headwinds like regulatory resets and inflationary pressures.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during the call, particularly in addressing productivity initiatives and growth projections. CFO Harry Taylor frequently highlighted Hydro One's track record of exceeding productivity expectations and achieving cost efficiencies.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained consistent, underscoring confidence in strategic initiatives and financial targets.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 saw a stronger emphasis on productivity savings and broadband opportunities compared to Q3. This was accompanied by an increase in capital investments, from $773 million in Q3 to $799 million.
  • EPS growth for 2027 was revised upward in Q4, compared to the 5%-7% annualized guidance reiterated in Q3.
  • Analysts in Q4 focused more on transmission project timelines and regulatory outcomes, whereas Q3 discussions leaned toward supply chain and inflation risks.
  • Management sentiment remained confident across both quarters; however, Q4 discussions highlighted tariff concerns and indigenous partnerships more prominently.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tariffs and trade tensions with the US were identified as potential risks, with ongoing monitoring and discussions to mitigate impacts.
  • Inflation and supply chain constraints remain challenges, though Hydro One has secured long-term vendor agreements to address these issues.
  • Regulatory resets in the next JRAP period could impact earnings, though management expressed confidence in tailwinds from new projects.

Final Takeaway

Hydro One delivered a strong performance in Q4 2024, with significant capital investments, increased customer satisfaction, and productivity savings driving results. Looking ahead, the company is poised for 6%-8% annual EPS growth through 2027, supported by strategic transmission projects and broadband investments. Management's commitment to cost efficiency and proactive regulatory engagement provides a solid foundation for sustained long-term growth, despite tariff and inflationary challenges.
